Description: Use proper <table> <thead> <tbody> <th> for data tables
Do: Use native HTML table elements — screen readers parse them correctly
Don't: Use CSS grid/flex to fake a table — SR can't parse it
Code_Bad: <div class='grid grid-cols-3'> for tabular data — invisible to SR
A11y_Note: scope='col' on <th>. caption element for table description.
Performance_Note: Native tables are the most performant for tabular data.

Description: Horizontal scroll wrapper for tables on small screens
Do: Wrap table in overflow-x-auto container. Show scroll shadow indicator.
Don't: Stack all columns vertically on mobile — loses row context
Code_Bad: <table class='hidden md:table'> + separate mobile card view — duplicates markup
A11y_Note: tabindex='0' on scroll container for keyboard scrolling
Performance_Note: Horizontal scroll is efficient — no DOM duplication

Description: Convert each row to a card on mobile screens
Do: Use data-label attributes to show column headers in card view
Don't: Card stack for 500+ rows — too many cards to scroll
Code_Bad: Mobile: show only 2 columns and hide the rest — user loses data
A11y_Note: Each card must be a complete row. Labels visible in card mode.
Performance_Note: Only suitable for small datasets. Cards = more DOM than table.

Description: Table header stays visible while scrolling long tables
Do: Use position:sticky on <thead> with top offset for sticky nav
Don't: Fixed header that detaches from table — column alignment breaks
Code_Good: thead { position: sticky; top: 64px; z-index: 10; background: white; }
Code_Bad:
Header clone
— alignment breaks on resize
A11y_Note: Sticky header keeps column context for all users
Performance_Note: CSS sticky is zero-JS and performant. No duplicate DOM.

Description: Click column header to sort ascending/descending/none
Do: Show sort indicator arrow. Cycle: none → asc → desc → none.
Don't: Sort on server for every click on paginated data — too slow for small sets
Code_Bad: <th onclick='sort()'> — th is not interactive; no sort indicator
A11y_Note: aria-sort='ascending|descending|none' on <th>. Use <button> inside.
Performance_Note: Client-side sort for <1000 rows. Server-side for larger sets.

Description: Drag column borders to resize column widths
Do: Persist column widths in localStorage per user/table
Don't: Reset column widths on every page reload — frustrating
Code_Bad: Fixed column widths with no resize — data gets truncated
A11y_Note: Ensure resize handle has min 44px target and keyboard support
Performance_Note: Use CSS resize or column-resize cursor. Minimal performance cost.

Description: Checkboxes for selecting multiple rows for bulk actions
Do: Show selected count + bulk action bar when rows selected
Don't: Enable selection but provide no bulk actions — pointless
Code_Bad: with no select-all or bulk actions
A11y_Note: Select-all checkbox in header. aria-label on each checkbox.
Performance_Note: Track selection state in Set for O(1) lookup performance.

Description: Click row to select/highlight for detail view
Do: Highlight selected row with distinct background. Show detail panel.
Don't: Mix click-to-select with click-to-navigate — confusing
Code_Bad: <tr onClick={navigate}> sometimes and <tr onClick={select}> other times
A11y_Note: aria-selected='true' on selected row. role='row' implicit in tr.
Performance_Note: Event delegation on <tbody> for click handler — don't attach per row.

Description: Filter controls in/below each column header
Do: Show filter input/dropdown per filterable column. Show active filter indicators
Don't: Hide filters behind modal — user can't see what's filtered
Code_Bad: Filters in separate page/modal — user loses table context
A11y_Note: Announce active filter count via aria-live region
Performance_Note: Client-side filter for <1000 rows. Debounce filter input 200ms.

Description: Single search box filtering across all columns
Do: Debounce 200ms. Highlight matching text in results. Show result count.
Don't: Search only one column — user expects global search
Code_Bad: rows.filter(r => r.name.includes(query)) — only searches name column
A11y_Note: Announce result count change via aria-live='polite'
Performance_Note: Client-side full-text search for <5000 rows. Use worker for larger.

Description: Show removable chips for each active filter above table
Do: Show chip per active filter with X to remove. Include 'Clear all' button.
Don't: Apply filters with no visible indicator of what's filtered — user forgets
Code_Bad: Filters applied but no visible indication — user confused why data is missing
A11y_Note: Each chip button needs aria-label='Remove filter: Status Active'
Performance_Note: Lightweight rendering. Chips are just spans with click handlers.

Description: Page size selector + page navigation below table
Do: Show rows-per-page selector (10/25/50/100) + page numbers + row count range
Don't: Show all 10000 rows on one page — browser freezes
Code_Bad:
Page 1
with no rows-per-page option — stuck with default
A11y_Note: aria-label on page size selector. Announce page change.
Performance_Note: Server-side pagination for >1000 rows. Include total count in API response.

Description: Render only visible rows in viewport for massive datasets
Do: Use virtualization library (TanStack Virtual/react-window) for 10K+ rows
Don't: Render all 50000 rows in DOM — browser crashes
Code_Bad: rows.map(r => <tr>...</tr>) for 50K rows — 50K DOM nodes
A11y_Note: Virtual tables break native table accessibility. Add role='grid'.
Performance_Note: Reduces DOM from 50K to ~30 visible nodes. Essential for large data.

Description: Truncate long cell text with ellipsis and show full text on hover
Do: Truncate with CSS text-overflow:ellipsis. Tooltip on hover/focus shows full text.
Don't: Truncate without any way to see full text — data is lost
Code_Bad: <td class='truncate'> — no way to read truncated content
A11y_Note: title attribute provides native tooltip. Add aria-label for full text.
Performance_Note: CSS truncation is zero-cost. Tooltips render on demand.

Description: Click cell to edit value directly in the table
Do: Click/Enter to edit. Escape to cancel. Tab to save and move to next cell.
Don't: Require separate edit form/modal for every field change — slow workflow
Code_Bad: Edit button per row opens modal form — 3 clicks per edit
A11y_Note: Announce edit mode via aria-live. Input needs aria-label with column name.
Performance_Note: Only render input for active cell — don't render all cells as inputs.

Description: Color-coded badge/pill showing row status
Do: Use color + text label + icon. Never color alone as indicator.
Don't: Use color alone for status — colorblind users can't distinguish
Code_Bad: — color only; no text
A11y_Note: Status text is accessible. Color alone violates WCAG 1.4.1.
Performance_Note: Badges are lightweight spans. No performance concern.

Description: Click row to expand detail section below it
Do: Show expand chevron. Expand pushes rows down — don't overlay.
Don't: Open detail in modal for every row — breaks table scanning flow
Code_Bad: Modal popup for row details — user can't compare rows
A11y_Note: aria-expanded on trigger. aria-controls pointing to detail panel.
Performance_Note: Only render expanded detail content on demand — not for all rows.

Description: Sticky bar appearing when rows selected showing bulk actions
Do: Show bar at bottom with selected count + actions (Delete/Export/Archive)
Don't: Put bulk actions in dropdown menu — user doesn't find them
Code_Good: 3 selected: [Delete] [Export]
Code_Bad: Bulk actions hidden in three-dot menu — low discoverability
A11y_Note: Announce selected count changes. Action buttons need clear labels.
Performance_Note: Bar renders conditionally — no cost when no selection.

Description: Select all rows including un-loaded pages
Do: Show banner: 'All 25 on this page selected. Select all 342 rows?' with action link.
Don't: Select all selects only visible page — user expects all matching results
Code_Bad: Select all checkbox only selects visible 25 of 342 — user unknowingly deletes only 25
A11y_Note: Announce selection scope change via aria-live
Performance_Note: Store 'select all' as flag — don't load all IDs into memory.

Description: Table state when no data matches filters or is empty
Do: Show illustration + message + clear suggestion (clear filters / add data)
Don't: Show blank table with just headers — user thinks page is broken
Code_Good: No results found.
Code_Bad: Empty <tbody> — just column headers with nothing below
A11y_Note: Empty state message in aria-live region
Performance_Note: No performance concern — single DOM element.

Description: Placeholder skeleton rows while data loads
Do: Show 5-10 skeleton rows matching actual column widths
Don't: Show spinner over empty table — no layout hint
Code_Bad: <div class='spinner'> centered over empty table
A11y_Note: aria-busy='true' on table during load. aria-live announces when ready.
Performance_Note: Skeleton CSS animation is GPU-accelerated. Lightweight.

Description: Show table structure immediately then load data progressively
Do: Render table frame + headers + skeleton instantly. Data fills in.
Don't: Wait for all data before showing any table — long blank screen
Code_Good: Render thead immediately → show skeleton tbody → replace with real data on fetch
Code_Bad: await fetchAll() then render entire table — 3s blank screen
A11y_Note: Announce data loaded via aria-live when skeleton replaced.
Performance_Note: First paint <200ms with skeleton. Data appears progressively.

Description: Allow users to export table data as file
Do: Export current filtered/sorted view — not raw data. Show progress for large exports.
Don't: Export all data when user has active filters — unexpected
Code_Bad: Export all 10K rows when user filtered to 50 — user gets wrong data
A11y_Note: Button is naturally accessible. Announce download started.
Performance_Note: Generate CSV client-side for <10K rows. Server-side for larger.

Description: Copy single row data to clipboard for quick sharing
Do: Right-click → Copy row or copy icon button per row
Don't: No way to copy individual row data — user manually selects text
Code_Bad: User manually triple-clicks to select cell text — unreliable
A11y_Note: Announce 'Copied to clipboard' via aria-live on success
Performance_Note: Clipboard API is async and lightweight.

Description: Show/hide columns via dropdown selector
Do: Persist column preferences. Show column count indicator.
Don't: Show 20 columns by default — overwhelming table width
Code_Bad: All 15 columns visible — table is 3000px wide; horizontal scroll needed
A11y_Note: Dropdown needs proper aria-haspopup and keyboard support
Performance_Note: Only render visible column TDs — reduces DOM per row.

Description: Pin first column(s) so they stay visible during horizontal scroll
Do: Pin ID/name column on left. Separator shadow on frozen column edge.
Don't: Freeze too many columns — main content area becomes too narrow
Code_Bad: <th style='position:fixed'> — completely breaks table layout
A11y_Note: Pinned columns maintain their th/td association
Performance_Note: CSS sticky is performant. No JS needed for basic pinning.

Description: Reduced row padding for data-dense views
Do: Offer density toggle: Comfortable / Compact / Dense
Don't: One size fits all — power users want dense; new users want comfortable
Code_Bad: Fixed padding with no density option — wastes space for power users
A11y_Note: Ensure touch targets remain ≥44px in compact mode
Performance_Note: Smaller rows = more visible data. Better perceived performance.

Description: Alternating row background colors for visual scanning
Do: Use subtle alternating backgrounds (white/gray-50). Disable in dark mode or adjust.
Don't: Heavy stripes with high contrast — distracting
Code_Bad: <tr style='background:rgba(0,0,0,0.1)'> on every other row — too dark
A11y_Note: Row striping helps track data across wide tables
Performance_Note: Zero cost — pure CSS nth-child selector.

Description: Drag rows to reorder them (priority lists/kanban)
Do: Show drag handle on left. Animate reorder. Save new order.
Don't: Enable drag on the entire row — conflicts with text selection
Code_Bad: <tr draggable='true'> — text selection broken; no drag handle
A11y_Note: Announce reorder changes via aria-live. Keyboard reorder with Alt+↑/↓
Performance_Note: Use requestAnimationFrame for smooth drag animation.

Description: Drag column headers to reorder columns
Do: Show drag preview of column. Snap to position. Persist order.
Don't: Allow reorder but reset on refresh — user does it again every time
Code_Bad: Column reorder but no persistence — frustrating repetition
A11y_Note: Announce column reorder completion via aria-live
Performance_Note: Reorder is a one-time operation — no ongoing performance cost.
